title: ornithopus sativus nothosubsp. macrorrhynchus
intro: Ornithopus sativus nothosubsp. macrorrhynchus, also known as the large-fruited broomclover, is a non-leguminous, perennial herb that belongs to the pea family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The large-fruited broomclover has been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Ornithopus sativus nothosubsp. macrorrhynchus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications.
